_schema_notes.coverage: Authoritative schema for all DID formats — DD Form 1664 (pre-2014) and free-text (post-2014 MIL-STD-963C)
_schema_notes.did_format: Set to 'dd_form_1664' or 'free_text'. Determines which fields are expected to be populated.
_schema_notes.963c_compliant: True if the DID was prepared under MIL-STD-963C (2014 or later). False for older DIDs.
_schema_notes.reserved_fields: Fields marked RESERVED are present in one format but not the other. Always include them — set to empty string or empty array when not applicable.
_schema_notes.preparation_instructions: Official MIL-STD-963C name for what was previously called 'requirements'. Contains the numbered sections of the DID. All section numbers are normalized to strictly numeric dot-notation (1, 1.1, 1.1.1). Letter-based source numbering (2.a, 3.1.b) is converted to numeric equivalents (2.1, 3.1.2).
_schema_notes.asterisk: True if the requirement number is followed by * in the source document (conditional requirement).

title: Security Classification GUIDE
summary: This Data Item Description specifies the format, content, and preparation instructions for a Security Classification Guide (SCG) as defined by OPNAVINST 5513.1F, crucial for identifying and protecting national security information.
use_relationship:
The Security Classification Guide (SCG), as defined by OPNAVINST 5513.1F, identifies information or components of a system, program, project, or multiple programs that, if exposed, are determined to be a risk to National security. SCGs serve both legal and management functions by recording Department original classification determinations made under Executive Order (EO) 13526 of 29 December 2009, and its predecessor and successor executive orders on national security information. SCGs are the primary reference source for derivative classifiers to identify the level and duration of classification for specific information elements. Three levels are assigned which are: top secret, secret, or confidential. This information is used to ensure there is an appropriate level of protection assigned and a time period for which this protection must be continued.

This Data Item Description contains the format, content, and preparation instruction for the data product resulting from the specific and discrete task requirement delineated in the contract.

OPNAVINST 5513.1F can be accessed at the Department of the Navy Issuances website:
(https://www.secnav.navy.mil/doni/opnav.aspx).

references.label: DoDM 5200.45 CH2, 15 SEPT 2020, "Instructions for Developing Security Classification Guides", with a reference to Encl 4

distribution_statement: DISTRIBUTION STATEMENT A: Approved for public release; distribution is unlimited.

preparation_instructions.content: The SCG shall be presented as specified in the contract. (DoDM 5200.45 CH2, 15 SEPT 2020, "Instructions for Developing Security Classification Guides", with a reference to Encl 4 (https://www.esd.whs.mil).
